title: Menu System
content:
# Menu System

If you use a blog to write documentation (like the page you are reading now), there is usually a menu bar/toc bar on the left to help with navigation. Kecare provides a simple and unified way to write menus.

---

## Menu Storage Location

In Kecare, menus have a fixed storage location:

| Item | Description |
|------|------|
| Directory Location | `theme directory/.kecare/menus/` |
| File Naming | `*.menu.source.ts` |

> ⚠️ **Note**: Only files placed in this directory can be scanned by Kecare to generate the corresponding menu.

---

## Creating Menu Files

### File Naming Rules

Menu files must adhere to the following naming format:

```txt
<菜单名称>.menu.source.ts
```

Example:

```txt
.kecare/menus/
├── kecare-docs.menu.source.ts    # Kecare 文档菜单
├── blog.menu.source.ts           # 博客菜单
└── api.menu.source.ts            # API 文档菜单
```

> 💡 **Tip**: The file name prefix (e.g., `kecare-docs`) will become the `menu` value you reference in the article's Front Matter.

---

## Writing Menu Content

### Basic Structure

Export the `navItems` array in the menu file:

```ts
// .kecare/menus/kecare-docs.menu.source.ts
import type { NavItem } from "kecare";

export const navItems: NavItem[] = [
    {
        text: "分组名称",
        level: 1,
        items: [
            {
                text: "菜单项",
                link: "./文章路径",
                level: 2,
            },
        ],
    },
];
```

### Complete Example

```ts
// .kecare/menus/kecare-docs.menu.source.ts
import type { NavItem } from "kecare";

export const navItems: NavItem[] = [
    {
        text: "开始使用",
        level: 1,
        items: [
            {
                text: "快速开始",
                link: "./快速开始",
                level: 2,
                desc: "5 分钟快速上手",
            },
            {
                text: "项目结构",
                link: "./项目结构约定",
                level: 2,
            },
            {
                text: "写作",
                link: "./写作",
                level: 2,
            },
        ],
    },
    {
        text: "核心功能",
        level: 1,
        items: [
            {
                text: "Front Matter",
                link: "./front-matter",
                level: 2,
            },
            {
                text: "Markdown 扩展",
                link: "./markdown扩展",
                level: 2,
            },
            {
                text: "国际化处理",
                link: "./国际化处理",
                level: 2,
            },
        ],
    },
    {
        text: "高级用法",
        level: 1,
        items: [
            {
                text: "自定义生成器",
                link: "./自定义生成器CLI",
                level: 2,
            },
            {
                text: "部署到 GitHub Pages",
                link: "./Github pages",
                level: 2,
            },
        ],
    },
];
```

---

## Enable Menu in Article

### Step 1: Add the menu field in the Front Matter

Add the `menu` field to the Front Matter of the article Markdown file where you need to display the menu:

```yaml
---
title: 快速开始
menu: kecare-docs    # 菜单 key
---
```

### Step 2: Ensure the Menu File Exists

The value of `menu` corresponds to the prefix of the menu file name:

| menu value | Corresponding menu file |
|---------|----------------|
| `kecare-docs` | `.kecare/menus/kecare-docs.menu.source.ts` |
| `blog` | `.kecare/menus/blog.menu.source.ts` |
| `api` | `.kecare/menus/api.menu.source.ts` |

### Rule Summary

```
menu 值 = .menu.source.ts 的文件名前缀
```

---

## NavItem Structure Description

### Type Definitions

Kecare's `NavItem` supports two node types:

```ts
type NavItem =
    | { text: string; link: string; level: number; desc?: string; icon?: string }  // 叶子节点
    | { text: string; items: NavItem[]; level: number };                           // 分组节点
```

### Leaf Node

Clickable menu item:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `text` | `string` | Yes | Display text |
| `link` | `string` | Yes | Navigation link |
| `level` | `number` | Yes | Hierarchy depth |
| `desc` | `string` | No | Description text (optional) |
| `icon` | `string` | No | Icon name (optional) |

### Group Nodes

For classification only, not clickable:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|------|------|
| `text` | `string` | Yes | Group name |
| `items` | `NavItem[]` | Yes | Submenu items |
| `level` | `number` | Yes | Hierarchy depth |

### Link Field Description

`link` refers to the path of the article to navigate to:

```ts
{
    text: "快速开始",
    link: "./快速开始",    // 相对路径
    level: 2,
}
```

Path Rules:
- Use relative path format: `./article-name`
- Corresponds to the route or slug of the article
- No file extension needed

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Q: Can an article be bound to multiple menus?

A: Typically, an article is bound to only one `menu`. If multiple menus are needed, support from the theme side is required (e.g., merging and rendering).

### Q: Must the menu file be named `*.menu.source.ts`?

A: Yes. This is the naming convention for Kecare scan menu files, and not following the rules may result in them not being recognized.

### Q: How to add menu icons?

A: Use the `icon` field:

```ts
{
    text: "快速开始",
    link: "./快速开始",
    level: 2,
    icon: "rocket",    // 图标名称
}
```

> 💡 **Tip**: Icon support depends on theme implementation.
